items.title: Why AppLovin (APP) Shares Are Plunging Today
items.description: Shares of mobile app technology company AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) fell 7.1% in the afternoon session after the company's stock fell amid a broader sell-off in the technology sector. The ad-tech company was one of the top decliners in the S&P 500. The drop happened as part of a wider retreat in the tech industry, which also saw shares of other notable firms like Micron, Lam Research, and Nvidia fall. This widespread decline pointed to a broader negative sentiment among investors toward the tech secto

items.title: 'That World Has Changed': Alex Gorsky on Shift to Regionalization
items.description: Alex Gorsky, board director for Apple, IBM, and JPMorgan Chase, discusses the end of the 30-year globalization era as businesses begin to take a more regionalized approach.

items.title: Apple Turns AI Caution Into Gold as Rivals Drown in Spending Spree
items.description: While Microsoft and Meta burn billions chasing AI dominance, Apple's restraint is suddenly its biggest strength.

items.title: Tech Weekly: precious chip deals, precious metals
items.description: STORY: From precious chip deals to precious metals.This is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyNvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company has seen "very strong demand" for its advanced Blackwell chips.And it's producing the semiconductors with the help of TSMC's wafers.Huang even said Nvidia's success wouldn't be possible without the Taiwanese firm's support on wafers.Nvidia made history last month when it became the first company to reach a $5 trillion market value.Apple won't release the next version of the iPhone Air in the fall of 2026 as previously planned.That's according to sources familiar with the matter.The company launched the iPhone Air in 2025 as a thinner and lighter alternative within its iPhone portfolio.The model came with some trade-offs in terms of battery size and camera features.CEO Elon Musk said Tesla will likely have to build a chip fab to make AI semiconductors.And he said the EV maker could work with U.S. firm Intel.Tesla is designing its fifth-generation AI chip to power its autonomous ambitions.Musk raised the issue at the company's annual meeting."As far as I can see, the only option is to go build some very big chip fab."The AA Driving School Academy says trainee driving instructors are turning to virtual reality to master complex road scenarios in a safe, controlled environment.Using Meta’s Quest 3 headsets, the driving school can introduce instructors to modern hazards.Including a higher number of cyclists and scooters.UK startup DEScycle is dissolving discarded tech using "deep eutectic solvent" chemistry to pull out precious and critical metals at room temperature.It's an alternative to energy-hungry smelting.And DEScycle says the liquids selectively dissolve metals from shredded circuit boards and other e‑waste while leaving most non‑metal components behind.

items.title: November Technology Portfolio Launches With Insights From Apple, PepsiCo, Vodafone, and Johnson Controls
items.description: The November editions of BizClik’s Technology portfolio are now live, featuring fresh insights from AI Magazine, Technology Magazine, Telco Magazine and Data Centre Magazine. This month’s issues spotlight global leaders, breakthrough technologies and the strategies shaping the future of AI, data and digital infrastructure.
